jd_text:
ABOUT BASETEN — Baseten powers mission-critical inference for the world's most dynamic AI companies (Cursor, Notion, OpenEvidence, Abridge, Clay, Gamma, Writer). Uniting applied AI research, flexible infrastructure, and seamless developer tooling. Recently raised a $300M Series E (BOND, IVP, Spark Capital, Greylock, Conviction).

THE ROLE — Hiring an Onboarding Program Manager to build and lead onboarding at Baseten, setting new hires up to do the best work of their careers. Ensure every person who joins (onboarding dozens of new hires each month) feels welcomed, builds early belonging, gains fluency in the product and how the company operates, and has clear expectations about success. This is NOT a logistics-only role. It sits at the intersection of learning & development, curriculum design, facilitation, and manager enablement. Own the onboarding journey from offer acceptance through the first 90 days and build scalable programs that accelerate ramp time, reinforce culture, and prepare new hires to contribute quickly in a highly technical environment.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
- Own the end-to-end onboarding experience: new hire journey from signed offer through first 90 days; design structured touch points (clarity, connection, momentum); welcome new hires in San Francisco on Day 1.
- Teach the story, culture, and product: design engaging ways to teach who Baseten is; bring culture to life via creative programming (executives, founders, customer stories); build and continuously improve onboarding curriculum across live and async formats; partner with subject matter experts to design/deliver training on highly technical concepts (e.g., deploying a model); develop specialized onboarding tracks for different teams and roles.
- Drive clarity and accelerate ramp time: equip managers with tools/guidance for role-specific onboarding; ensure every new hire has clear expectations, defined success metrics, and meaningful early wins; design onboarding for velocity (engineers ship code within days, present at Show and Tell within weeks); accelerate ownership.
- Build programs that scale: partner with People Ops, IT, and Workplace Experience for seamless Day 1 readiness; manage program logistics (scheduling, coordination, attendance tracking); own onboarding infrastructure including LMS tools and systems for live/async learning; track engagement and outcomes, collect feedback, continuously iterate.

REQUIREMENTS:
- 7+ years of experience in onboarding, learning & development, or enablement.
- Strong curriculum design and content development across live and async formats.
- Excellent facilitator (technical and non-technical audiences).
- Experience partnering with subject matter experts to translate complex concepts into clear, practical learning.
- Builder mentality, high ownership, bias toward execution.
- Strong written communicator.
- Experience in a fast-growing startup environment; comfortable with speed and ambiguity.
- Deeply human approach; care about how people feel, not just what they learn.
- Passionate about making learning valuable, practical, worth people's time.

LOCATION: San Francisco (Hybrid), Full time. Department: G&A / People / People Operations. Compensation: $160K-$200K + equity. EEO employer; SF Fair Chance Ordinance noted. No visa/sponsorship language stated either way.

key_points.function: EMPLOYEE / PEOPLE onboarding (HR / People Operations) — CONFIRMED. Department is G&A > People > People Operations. The role builds new-hire onboarding from offer acceptance through the first 90 days. This is NOT customer/post-sales onboarding.
key_points.yoe_required: 7+ years in onboarding, L&D, or enablement
must_haves: Strong facilitation for technical + non-technical audiences
Partner with SMEs to translate complex/technical concepts into practical learning
Builder mentality, high ownership, bias to execution
Fast-growing startup experience; comfort with ambiguity
Own onboarding infrastructure incl. LMS tools; track engagement/outcomes/feedback
Manager enablement (equip managers with role-specific onboarding tools)
key_points.sponsorship_signal: unknown — JD states no sponsorship language either way. Not an explicit no-sponsor hard stop, but Baseten is not a confirmed H1B sponsor in jamie/h1b_verified.md and is not cap-exempt.

fit: FUNCTION: Employee/people onboarding (HR) — confirmed, NOT customer onboarding. This is the purest functional match in Jamie's P1b sweet spot: onboarding + L&D + curriculum design + facilitation + manager enablement is exactly her day-to-day. Her real track record maps directly: InGenius (redesigned onboarding for 70+ cross-functional staff, cut onboarding time 75%, built SOPs + self-service knowledge base, facilitated training for 600+, ran needs assessments with 25+ stakeholders); Vestas (optimized onboarding across 3 global locations across the employee lifecycle); NextGen + ODN (curriculum/L&D + diagnostic). VERDICT-GATE: the 7+ YOE requirement is the disqualifier. Jamie has ~3 years. Per profile_compact.md YOE cap (~5yr max, +2 makeable) and feedback_cap_yoe_stretch_dig_for_fit.md, 6-8+ year asks are TOO FAR and should not get submit-energy unless there is a real offsetting advantage (cap-exempt + perfect scope, OR a warm intro). Baseten is neither cap-exempt nor a confirmed sponsor, and there is no warm intro on file. The calibration reference list explicitly cites 7yr roles (Coinbase Sr PM L&D, Chainalysis People Programs Lead 6yr) as TOO-FAR-going-forward — this Baseten role is in that same band. Recommendation: DO NOT bulk-submit. Package built per request for the warm-intro exception path only (if Jamie can secure an alumni/referral intro, the scope fit is strong enough to justify pursuing despite the YOE gap). Secondary flag: no confirmed sponsorship + SF hybrid (Day 1 in-person) adds friction for a remote-PNW candidate needing relocation + visa.
